title: RenodeServer<->Simulation time-model mismatch (BARRIER_SYNC stalls at t=0; duplicate RX delivery)
description:
## Problem

RenodeServer uses non-blocking RX_POLL, which never puts the node into RX_WAIT state. In BARRIER_SYNC mode, `maybe_advance_time()` only advances when a node is in RX_WAIT, so sim time stays at 0. Result: transmissions never expire, same packet re-delivered 80+ times.

## Solution: Interrupt-driven push

Replace polling with push-based RX that matches real SX1262 hardware:

```
# Firmware issues SetRx (0x82) → SX1262.cs sends:
Renode → Sim: RX_ENTER (0x24) + timeout_us

# Sim puts node in RX_WAIT, BARRIER_SYNC can advance time
# When packet arrives (or timeout), sim pushes:
Sim → Renode: RX_PACKET (0x27) + payload + rssi + snr
         -or- RX_TIMEOUT (0x28)

# SX1262.cs raises DIO1 → firmware ISR fires
# Firmware issues SetStandby (0x80) → SX1262.cs sends:
Renode → Sim: RX_EXIT (0x26)
```

## Why this works

1. RX_ENTER puts node in RX_WAIT → BARRIER_SYNC unblocked
2. Sim pushes exactly once per packet (no duplicates)  
3. DIO1 interrupt matches real hardware behavior
4. No polling = no wasted cycles = battery-friendly model

## Acceptance Criteria

- 2-node Renode mesh in BARRIER_SYNC mode delivers each frame exactly once
- Receiving firmware logs inbound frame via DIO1 ISR path
- No RX_POLL in protocol (deleted)
acceptance_criteria: Documented time-model choice for the Renode bridge; a 2-node Renode run delivers each frame exactly once to the receiving firmware.
close_reason: Fixed: Added background simulation driver task to RenodeServer that calls deliver_pending_packets() and maybe_advance_time() periodically. This ensures BARRIER_SYNC mode advances time correctly and packets are delivered exactly once. Also added MSG_RX_ENTER/MSG_RX_EXIT handling to NodeServer for compatibility with push-based SimRadio client. All 758 simulation tests pass.
